title: Could use some help with meshcore app and firmware

messages.author: Vinny | Creator
messages.text: Working on https://github.com/STCisGOOD/yours-x-lunarcore which is an encrypted P2P messaging app for Android that runs over MeshCore via LoRa. The firmware side is https://github.com/STCisGOOD/lunarcore which is a multi-protocol ESP32-S3 firmware that auto-detects MeshCore/Meshtastic/Reticulum. Looking for help with a few things: Bug: Messages send fine one direction but the other device can't respond back. Both devices receive the initial message but the return path seems to break. If anyone's run into something similar with MeshCore packet routing I'd appreciate any pointers. Testing: If you've got a Heltec V3 and want to try it out, both repos are open source. Would love reports from different environments/distances. Coverage in Brooklyn: Planning to set up repeater/relay nodes on rooftops around Brooklyn running this stack. Goal is borough-wide LoRa coverage for off-grid messaging. Also want to use the same node network to broadcast a radio station over LoRa . If you're in the area or have experience with rooftop deployments, hit me up. Also if you have advice on the repeater/relay node architecture/hardware, PLEASE hit me up. I really need all the advice i can get here. Also here's a demo video of the "YOURS" app running on android: https://x.com/stcisgood/status/2028926459159437436 . You cant see it but at 1:10ish i plug the phones into two separate Heltec V3s running the lunarcore firmware. Ok, cool, thanks!
